text.title: integration-testing Failure Modes
text.summary: Four antipatterns each with symptom / root-cause / fix.

antipattern.description: Mocking the ORM session / repository in "integration" tests.
antipattern.symptom: Tests pass but production ORM queries fail; schema migrations untested; uniqueness constraints fire only in prod.
antipattern.root-cause: Treating integration as "unit + a stub" — defeats the purpose of integration.
antipattern.fix: Replace mocks with a Testcontainers-backed real session. Move mock-heavy tests to tests/unit/.

antipattern.symptom: Suite suddenly fails after Docker Hub publishes a new major; CI flakes for unrelated reasons.
antipattern.fix: Pin to a specific version (postgres:16.2). Update in a separate PR with full suite green.

antipattern.description: Spinning a fresh container per test instead of per session.
antipattern.symptom: Suite takes minutes; CI runtime balloons; container startup costs dwarf the actual tests.
antipattern.root-cause: Container fixture scope set to function/module instead of session.
antipattern.fix: Container fixture scope="session". Per-test isolation via transaction-rollback session fixture, not new container.

antipattern.description: Cleanup via teardown delete (`DELETE FROM users` in fixture teardown) instead of transaction rollback.
antipattern.symptom: Test failures leave orphan rows; later tests fail on unique constraints; manual DB cleanup needed.
antipattern.root-cause: Teardown only runs on success path; failures skip it.
antipattern.fix: Begin transaction in fixture; ROLLBACK in finally/yield-exit. Atomic, survives crashes.
